items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'food safety danger zone'? (set 2)
items.explanation: food safety danger zone corresponds to 5C to 60C.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is '1 teaspoon in metric volume'? (set 2)
items.explanation: 1 teaspoon in metric volume corresponds to 5 mL.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'to cook uncovered with dry heat and fat'? (set 2)
items.explanation: to cook uncovered with dry heat and fat corresponds to roast.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'quick high-heat cooking in a pan with little oil'? (set 2)
items.explanation: quick high-heat cooking in a pan with little oil corresponds to sauteing.

items.q: Resting meat always makes it drier.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'emulsion of oil and vinegar'? (set 2)
items.explanation: emulsion of oil and vinegar corresponds to vinaigrette.

items.q: In kitchen basics, what is 'resting cooked meat does what'?
items.explanation: The expected answer is redistributes juices.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'simmering temperature range'? (set 2)
items.explanation: simmering temperature range corresponds to about 85C to 96C.

items.q: FIFO is a stock rotation method in kitchens.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'adding salt to boiling pasta water does what'? (set 2)
items.explanation: adding salt to boiling pasta water does what corresponds to seasons pasta.
